authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(94) — Milk Ranch Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.)
sources.quotedText: (94) Milk Ranch Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Landforms layer record for gaz_id 263579, "Milk Ranch Meadow", Flat, Alpine Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":263579,"gaz_name":"Milk Ranch Meadow","gaz_featureclass":"Flat","fcode":15028,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Alpine","objectid_1":71098}; point (Web Mercator -13338972.1866, 4650605.7689) converts to lon -119.826026, lat 38.502137. GNIS has no distinct stream-type record for "Milk Ranch Creek" in Alpine Co. (only unrelated Humboldt/Trinity/Plumas Co. creeks of the same name); this same-named meadow is the nearest authoritative GNIS anchor, ~1.4 km from the North Fork Mokelumne River's upper reach (GNIS gaz_id 272696), consistent with the CCR description of Milk Ranch Creek's confluence with that river. Corrects prior file coordinates (-119.8790, 38.5030), which were off by ~4.6 km from this point.

parameters.note: Milk Ranch Creek and its tributaries, above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River, are closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River
regulations.verbatimText: Milk Ranch Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River. Closed to all fishing all year.

start.verbatim: Closed to all fishing all year

end.verbatim: Closed to all fishing all year

parameters.note: No open season; the water is closed to fishing at all times.

regulations.humanSummary: No open fishing season — closed all year
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Not applicable at this water since fishing is closed all year, but retained as the standing statewide requirement.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
